text: At the very beginning, God created the sky and the earth.
The earth had no shape and was empty, darkness covered the deep waters, and God's Spirit hovered above the surface of the water.
God said, let there be light, and light appeared.
God saw that the light was good, and he separated the light from the darkness.
God named the light Day and the darkness Night; evening came, then morning — the first day.
God said, let there be a space between the waters, dividing water from water.
So God made the space and separated the water above it from the water below it, and it was so.
God named the space Sky; evening came, then morning — the second day.
God said, let the water under the sky gather into one place, and let dry ground appear, and it was so.
God named the dry ground Land and the gathered water Seas, and he saw that it was good.
God said, let the land grow plants: seed-bearing plants and fruit trees bearing fruit with seed inside, each after its own kind, and it was so.
The land produced vegetation, plants with seed of their own kind, and trees bearing fruit with seed inside of their own kind, and God saw that it was good.
Evening came, then morning — the third day.
God said, let there be lights in the sky to divide day from night, marking seasons, days, and years,
and let them shine from the sky to give light on the earth, and it was so.
God made the two great lights, the larger one to rule the day and the smaller one to rule the night, and the stars as well.
God set them in the sky to give light to the earth,
to govern the day and the night and to divide light from darkness, and God saw that it was good.
Evening came, then morning — the fourth day.
God said, let the waters swarm with living creatures, and let birds fly above the earth across the sky.
So God created the great sea creatures and every living thing that moves and swarms in the water, each of its own kind, and every winged bird of its own kind, and God saw that it was good.
God blessed them, saying, be fruitful and multiply and fill the waters of the seas, and let the birds multiply on the land.
Evening came, then morning — the fifth day.
God said, let the land produce living creatures of every kind: livestock, small crawling creatures, and wild animals, each of its own kind, and it was so.
God made the wild animals, the livestock, and everything that crawls on the ground, each of its own kind, and God saw that it was good.
God said, let us make humankind in our image, resembling us, so they may rule over the fish of the sea, the birds of the sky, the livestock, and every crawling creature on the earth.
So God created humankind in his own image, in the image of God he created them; he made them male and female.
God blessed them and told them to be fruitful and multiply, fill the earth and govern it, and rule over the fish, the birds, and every living creature that moves on the ground.
God said, I am giving you every seed-bearing plant on the whole earth and every tree with seed-bearing fruit — this will be your food.
And to every animal of the earth, every bird of the sky, and every crawling creature with the breath of life in it, I give every green plant for food, and it was so.
God looked at everything he had made, and it was very good indeed; evening came, then morning — the sixth day.
